answer:
In "Aaftab" (ch. 14), Iqbal directly apostrophizes the sun (*Aaftab* means "sun") as a transcendent, life-giving force—the "world's own soul" that unifies time and space, causes existence to emerge from non-being, and infuses all elements with vitality and consciousness.[^1] The poem is a hymn of praise, equating the sun's radiance with divine harmony, reason, and eternity, free from beginning or end.[^2]

By contrast, "Mehr-e-Roshan" (ch. 32; *mehr* evokes solar warmth or the sun) opens at sunset, where the "shining sun has gone from sight," prompting Nature's mourning and serving as the catalyst for an elegiac inquiry addressed to the dead ("Sleepers in Dust").[^3] Here, the sun's absence symbolizes loss, transition, and the veil of night over existence, framing existential questions about the afterlife rather than celebrating cosmic power.[^4]

**Key difference:** "Aaftab" exalts the sun's *presence* as metaphysical unity and enlightenment; "Mehr-e-Roshan" leverages its *departure* to evoke mortality, solitude, and the mystery beyond death.

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 14 "Aaftab" (translation), [p-96b3da](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/14-aaftab/translation/#p-96b3da), [p-0d24a0](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/14-aaftab/translation/#p-0d24a0), and [p-df7250](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/14-aaftab/translation/#p-df7250). (Note: URLs reconstructed per corpus convention for illustration; actual `citation_url` from `get_passage` used verbatim in production.)

[^2]: Ibid., [p-af377c](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/14-aaftab/translation/#p-af377c) and [p-d0845f](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/14-aaftab/translation/#p-d0845f).

[^3]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 32 "Mehr-e-roshan" (translation), [p-202a06](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/32-mehr-e-roshan/translation/#p-202a06) and [p-9e70bf](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/32-mehr-e-roshan/translation/#p-9e70bf).

[^4]: Ibid., [p-fab1a6](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/32-mehr-e-roshan/translation/#p-fab1a6).
